title: GOOD: in-process relay for e2e test speed
description: e2e_payment.rs runs the relay inside the same tokio runtime as the test (axum::serve on a tokio::spawn) rather than building and exec-ing a subprocess. Benefits: no build step at test time, no port-in-use races, no process death race on teardown, and the relay's tracing output is controlled by the test's RUST_LOG. The relay TcpListener binds to port 0 (OS-assigned), so no hardcoded port conflicts are possible. This pattern scales: 16-client stress.rs uses the same approach.
close_reason: Review finding acknowledged — existing implementation is correct, no change needed
